claim_boundary: Layer-3 scan scheduling receipt only. It uses existing inventory and transfer receipts to choose next bounded probes. It does not decode chain semantics, claim compression gain, claim market prediction, or fetch additional corpus bytes.

scan_actions.reason: Remote payload/parquet count is below inventory object count.
Use deterministic numeric residuals as route priors before byte-heavy scans.
The scan output becomes the next L3 control input.

actions.reason: Blockchain records can contain intentional public data carriers; scan them as storage surfaces before treating bytes as neutral noise.

channels.use_boundary: Route by byte density and selector shape only; no private-key, exploit, or evasion workflow.

global_storage_boundary: Public blockchains can store data, but this scheduler only models storage-bearing surfaces for detection, provenance, and compression-route diagnostics. Bulk payload reconstruction and payload publication remain outside this receipt.
